sutta: monks pursuing 5 courses a clan going among becomes among clan folk neither dear nor loved nor revered nor what he ought to become what 5 he is intimate with those who are unfamiliar interfering without warren frequents the society of dissenters whispers in the ear and asks too much monks pursuing these 5 courses a clan going is not dear nor loved nor revered to clan vote that's the end of the suta
commentary: that means among should be careful not to do these few things now the first 1 is to be too intimate too friendly with those who are unfamiliar probably newcomers they're too familiar too close to too friendly with newcomers those who are unfamiliar people would suspect something wrong second 1 interfering without warren that means interfering probably in lay people's affairs without a good reason interfering in lay people's affairs even even if it's not asked third 1 frequents the society of dissenters that means those of wrong views and those of other views a man keeps going to them also people think there's something wrong the fourth 1 whispers in the ear that means especially if a monk talks to a woman and he talks and whispers very softly and all that people think he's saying some things that he should not be saying and that he doesn't want other people to hear and people will suspect he's uh um i think words that are indecent or improper and the fifth 1 he asked too much that means he asked the lay people to give him things much more than is reasonable and the next utah is
